text.title: Blurred Roles and Team Evolution Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed across deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: PM and design lead both produce conflicting specs; eng ships the loudest.
antipattern.root-cause: No DRI assigned to the PM∩Design overlap zone.
antipattern.fix: Assign a single DRI per Venn zone; agents may participate but never own the decision.

antipattern.symptom: PMs expected to give design feedback; designers not expected to track metrics; designers resent the blur.
antipattern.root-cause: One-sided blurring is extractive; expectations were never paired.
antipattern.fix: Pair every cross-expectation symmetrically; document in the role-evolution doc.

antipattern.symptom: No depth specialist in security; quarterly audit surfaces 12 critical gaps.
antipattern.root-cause: Team blurred security across all engineers; nobody owned depth.
antipattern.fix: Retain at least one depth specialist per deep domain (security, ML eval, a11y); blur the periphery only.

antipattern.symptom: Team hires 2 PMs per engineer because of Ng 2024 talk; engineers report being micromanaged.
antipattern.root-cause: Treated ratio as a universal target rather than per-team hypothesis.
antipattern.fix: Measure agent-assisted velocity per team; tune ratio per measured throughput, not slideware.
